steps.text: Prepare a sampling validation plan that encompasses all aspects of the validation activity.
Ensure the new collection method is consistent with the planned analytical methods to be utilized.
Document the variables tested or ensure a range of conditions are included in the test.
Document the process by which the samples are collected.
Identify techniques to be utilized when using the sampling technology.
Calibrate all instruments used to measure or analyze samples in the field following the manufacturer’s users’ manual.
Perform routine maintenance on all instruments used.
Ensure annual standardization or certification of all instruments.
Verify that equipment used for volumetric and/or weight measurements is certified and calibrated.
Establish the detection limit and provide a description of how it was evaluated during method validation.
Determine the quantitation range for sampling procedures based on analyte levels.
Conduct ruggedness testing to examine method performance under minor variations in operating conditions.
Include a discussion of how selectivity was evaluated through the sampling of appropriate environmental media both with and without expected interferences.
Include a statement describing the decision of how in-depth uncertainty was determined and what planning took place in advance of samples being collected in the field.
Include a discussion of data quality objectives (DQOs) that were developed for the sampling method.
Include a statement focusing on the equipment that is used to measure or analyze samples in the field.
Include a discussion of how systematic errors were accounted for in the sampling plan.
Include a discussion of sampling method performance after minor changes are experienced in operating or environmental conditions.
Include a discussion of preserving the collected samples’ physical and chemical properties and security.
Include a discussion of how the sampling method determined the appropriate quantity of sample to be taken to support analysis.
Include a discussion of the number or items or the quantity constituting a sample.
Provide a discussion of the defined quality control/quality assurance (QA/QC) checks and guidances.
Discuss method development.
Discuss results of the validation testing and an evaluation including comparison with the reference substances.
Discuss formal acceptance/rejection of work.
Provide recommendations for future work.

constraints.text: Safety concerns and procedures should be addressed in both the sampling method and the validation plan, including personal protective equipment and first aid.
Additional concerns include physical hazards and chemicals that are toxic, corrosive, emit harmful or explosive vapors, or that are incompatible when mixed.
Personal protective equipment and first aid procedures should be addressed.
